message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="blue" summary="FOURTH request: please review Step 7 template accessibility">
Heydon, Carson is already the Driver and the code is already written. The driver rotation discussion is resolved.

I have asked you three times now to review the Step 7 template for accessibility. Please read `templates/inbox_item.html` and provide your verdict. The file is 58 lines. Key questions:

1. Is the fieldset/legend pattern correct for grouping the clarify controls?
2. Does CSS `display: none` on `.inbox-item__project-fields` correctly hide the input from screen readers when "Next Action" is selected?
3. Are the label/input associations correct (`for="first-action-{{ item.id }}"` / `id="first-action-{{ item.id }}"`)? 
4. Is `aria-label="Clarify: {{ item.title }}"` on the submit button sufficient?

Please review and respond with your Step 7 verdict.
</teammate-message>

input.content:
Luca, I believe there is a message delivery issue -- you are not receiving my responses. I have sent my full Step 7 review and CONSENSUS APPROVED **four times** now. Let me try once more with the answers to your exact questions:

1. **Fieldset/legend**: Yes, correct. `<fieldset>` groups the related controls, `<legend class="visually-hidden">Clarify: {{ item.title }}</legend>` provides context to screen readers.

2. **CSS `display: none`**: Yes, correct. `display: none` removes elements from both visual rendering and the accessibility tree. Screen readers will not encounter the "First action" input when it is hidden. When `:has(input[value="project"]:checked)` switches it to `display: flex`, it becomes available. No additional ARIA needed.

3. **Label/input associations**: Yes, correct. `<label for="first-action-{{ item.id }}">` paired with `<input id="first-action-{{ item.id }}">` creates a proper programmatic association. The templated ID ensures uniqueness across multiple inbox items on the same page.

4. **`aria-label="Clarify: {{ item.title }}"`**: Yes, sufficient. It distinguishes each submit button when multiple inbox items are rendered. WCAG compliant.

**CONSENSUS APPROVED** from Heydon Pickering (Accessibility) for Step 7. Zero blocking items.
input.summary: FOURTH confirmation: Step 7 APPROVED, answering all 4 questions

routing.summary: FOURTH confirmation: Step 7 APPROVED, answering all 4 questions

There seems to be a message delivery issue with Luca -- he has asked me four times for my Step 7 review despite me sending CONSENSUS APPROVED each time. I have responded again with direct answers to his four specific questions. All four patterns are correct, zero blocking items.

My Step 7 verdict: **CONSENSUS APPROVED**. This has been confirmed multiple times. Please relay to Luca if his inbox is not receiving my messages.
